questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Captivity
questions.explanation: D)Captivity(noun): The condition of being imprisoned or confined, imprisonment, enslavement. कैद, बंदी Antonym:Liberty(noun): The state of being free within society from oppressive restrictions, freedom, independence. स्वतंत्रता, आज़ादी Ethnicity(noun): The fact or state of belonging to a social group with a common national or cultural tradition, race, or heritage. जातीयता, नस्लीयता Entity (noun):A thing with distinct and independent existence, being, presence. अस्तित्व, इकाई Authenticity (noun):The quality of being genuine or true, originality, reliability. प्रामाणिकता, वास्तविकता

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A room in a hospital used especially for sunbathing or therapeutic exposure to light.
questions.explanation: B)Solarium(noun) – A room in a hospital used especially for sunbathing or therapeutic exposure to light सौर कक्ष Sanatorium(noun) – An establishment for the medical treatment of people who are convalescent or have a chronic illness चिकित्सालय Planetarium(noun) – A building in which images of stars, planets, and constellations are projected onto a dome for public entertainment or education तारामंडल Podium(noun) – A small platform on which a person may stand to be seen by an audience मंच

questions.question: In the following sentence the underlined part contains as error. Alternatives to the underlined part are given as options. Select the correct alternative. He is truest to his words.
questions.explanation: C) 'truest' के बदले'true'का use होगा क्योंकि ''trueto his words' phrase एक idiomatic expression है जिसका अर्थ होता है "to do what you say you will do, or to keep your promises" (जो कहा है वह करना या अपने वादों को निभाना)। अतः यहां 'true' ही सही है। उदाहरण के लिए— He is true to his promise. 'true'will be used instead of 'truest' because the phrase 'true to one's words' is an idiomatic expression. Therefore, 'true' is the correct word here. For example— He is true to his promise.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Tranquil
questions.explanation: A)Tranquil(adjective) – Calm, peaceful, serene, placid, still. शांत Antonym:Unquiet(adjective) – Not calm or peaceful, restless, agitated, noisy. अशांत Composed(adjective) – Calm, collected, unflustered, self-possessed. संयमित Serene(adjective) – Calm, peaceful, tranquil, placid. शांत Shabby(adjective) – Worn-out, dilapidated, ragged, neglected. जीर्ण-शीर्ण

questions.question: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. (A) to become severely seasick (B) a world tour, but he was (C) a nervous flyer and tended (D) he wanted to go on
questions.explanation: D)DBCA he wanted to go on a world tour, but he was a nervous flyer and tended to become severely seasick

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: D) The correct spelling isParliament, which refers to "the supreme legislative body of a country, especially in systems with a monarchy or democratic governance." it means संसद.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. The documentary presented a sobering view of the environmental damage caused by industrial waste.
questions.explanation: C) Sobering(adjective) – Serious, Alarming, Thought-provoking, Grave, Humbling गंभीर Synonym: Serious(adjective) – Grave, sober, solemn, earnest, severe गंभीर Reaction(noun) – A response to some treatment, situation, or stimulus. प्रतिक्रिया Proved(verb) – Demonstrated the truth or existence of something through evidence or argument. सिद्ध करना Researches(noun) – Systematic investigation into a subject in order to discover facts, to establish or revise a theory, or to develop a plan of action based on the facts discovered. अनुसंधान

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the spelling of the underlined word in the given sentence. The police informed the statesman that the goons were up to something mischievious
questions.explanation: D) The correct spelling of ‘mischievious’is ‘mischievous’, which means “showing a fondness for causing trouble in a playful or teasing way” (शरारती, नटखट).

questions.question: Select the appropriate option to fill in the blank. I haven't seen him _______ last week.
questions.explanation: B)'Since'का use सही है क्योंकि यह किसी निश्चित point of time को दर्शाता है, जैसे 'last week'। Sentence में बताया गया है कि वक्ता ने पिछले हफ्ते से उस व्यक्ति को नहीं देखा है, और 'since' इस समय बिंदु को सही तरीके से जोड़ता है। 'To' का use movement या direction को दिखाने के लिए होता है, जो यहां contextually गलत है। 'For' duration (अवधि) को दर्शाने के लिए use होता है, लेकिन यहाँ duration की बजाय specific point of time दिया गया है। 'Beyond' का अर्थ होता है "के पार" या "से परे," जो इस context में grammatically और contextually सही नहीं है। 'Since'is correct because it refers to a specific point of time, like 'last week.' The sentence indicates the speaker hasn’t seen the person from a specific point in time, and 'since' appropriately connects this idea. 'To' is used for direction or movement, which is incorrect in this context. 'For' indicates a duration, but the sentence uses a point of time, not a period. 'Beyond' means "further than" or "outside the limits," which is neither grammatically nor contextually correct here.

questions.question: Select the best express synonym of the given underlined word. Despite the earthquake's severity, the ancient temple remained intact, with no signs of damage to its structure.
questions.explanation: B) Intact(adjective) – Whole, Unbroken, Undamaged, Complete, Unharmedअक्षुण्ण/ज्यों का त्यों Synonym: Unbroken(adjective) – Whole, intact, complete, continuous, unimpaired अखंड Shattered(adjective) – Broken, smashed, fragmented, destroyed, wrecked टूटा हुआ Fragmented(adjective) – Broken up, divided, shattered, fragmented, splintered विखंडित Separated(adjective) – Divided, parted, detached, isolated, segregated अलग किया हुआ

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the highlighted group of words in the given sentence. We had to study a poem of lamentation, especially for the dead, written by Thomas Gray.
questions.explanation: D)Elegy(noun) – A poem of lamentation, especially for the dead. शोक-गीत Essay(noun) – A short piece of writing on a particular subject. निबंध Account(noun) – A report or description of an event or experience. विवरण Report(noun) – A formal statement or account describing an event or situation. प्रतिवेदन

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Callous
questions.explanation: D)Callous(adjective) – Showing or having an insensitive and cruel disregard for others; unfeeling, insensitive, heartless. निर्दयी, कठोर Antonym:Tender(adjective) – Showing gentleness, kindness, and concern; soft, delicate, compassionate. कोमल, दयालु Beguile(verb) – To charm or enchant someone in a deceptive way; deceive, mislead. छल करना Augment(verb) – To increase, add to, or make greater in size or amount; enhance. बढ़ाना Desolate(adjective) – Barren, deserted, or feeling utterly alone and hopeless; forlorn. उजाड़, अकेला

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Abundant
questions.explanation: C)Abundant(adjective) – Existing in large quantities, plentiful, bountiful, overflowing. प्रचुर मात्रा में, बहुतायत में। Synonym:Ample(adjective) – Enough or more than enough, plentiful, abundant, sufficient. पर्याप्त या बहुतायत में। Skimpy(adjective) – Insufficient in quantity or quality, meager, scanty.अल्प, अपर्याप्त। Elusive(adjective) – Difficult to find, catch, or achieve, evasive. पकड़ में न आने वाला, मुश्किल से मिलने वाला। Rare(adjective) – Uncommon or infrequent, scarce. दुर्लभ, बहुत कम मिलने वाला।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Head in the clouds
options: Thinking about the sky and clouds
questions.explanation: A)Head in the clouds(idiom) – Being impractical अव्यावहारिक होना

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. Seizing the opportunity, Akhil decided to take the bull with the horns
options: take the bull at the horns
take the bull by the horns
take the bull on the horns
take the bull in the horns
questions.explanation: B)take the bull by the horns– To face a difficult situation boldly. कठिन परिस्थिति का साहसपूर्वक सामना करना। 'take the bull by the horns' के बदले 'take the bull with the horns' का प्रयोग गलत है। सही वाक्यांश 'take the bull by the horns' है, जो कि एक प्रसिद्ध मुहावरा है और इसका अर्थ होता है— किसी समस्या या चुनौती का साहसपूर्वक और सीधे सामना करना। 'take the bull with the horns' should be replaced with 'take the bull by the horns' because the correct idiom is "take the bull by the horns," which means to face a problem or challenge boldly and directly.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. It's raining cats and dogs.
options: It's a perfect day for a picnic.
there are cats and dogs in the rain.
questions.explanation: B)It's raining cats and dogs (Idiom)– It's raining very heavily बहुत तेज बारिश होना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom that can complete the given sentence. Recent research says that most of our cottage industries lack finance. Thus, they are ____________.
questions.explanation: B)Thrown out of gear(idiom) – To stop functioning properly or face disruption.सही ढंग से काम न करना या बाधित होना Under a cloud(idiom): Suspicious or not trusted; under doubt. संदेह के घेरे में Taking somebody for a ride(idiom): To deceive or cheat someone.किसी को धोखा देना Worn and torn(phrase): Damaged or exhausted due to excessive use. घिसा-पिटा या जर्जर

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. They was / celebrating their / Silver wedding anniversary / next month in Dubai
questions.explanation: C)"They" plural subject है, इसलिए इसके साथ plural verb "were" का use होना चाहिए। "was" singular verb है और plural subject के साथ मेल नहीं खाती। "They was"contains the grammatical error The subject "They" is plural, so the verb must agree with it. The correct verb form is "were," not "was," because "was" is singular and does not match the plural subject "They." Corrected Sentence: "They were celebrating their Silver wedding anniversary next month in Dubai."

questions.question: The given sentence has an error. Select the option that rectifies the error. The students were asked to turn up their assignments by Friday
questions.explanation: B) सही वाक्य में "turn in" का use होना चाहिए, क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है submit करना । वाक्य का सही रूप होगा: "The students were asked to turn in their assignments by Friday." sentence में "turn up" का use गलत है। इसका अर्थ है "किसी स्थान पर आना" या "दिखाई देना," जो यहां context के लिए उपयुक्त नहीं है। The correct phrase is "turn in," which means "to submit." The correct sentence will be: "The students were asked to turn in their assignments by Friday." The phrase "turn up"is incorrect here. It means "to appear" or "to arrive," which is not contextually appropriate.

questions.question: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. (A) and was searching for them (B) the grandmother couldn’t find (C) all around the house (D) placed them on her head (E) her spectacles because she had
questions.explanation: A) BEDAC the grandmother couldn’t find her spectacles because she had placed them on her head and was searching for them all around the houseB starts the sentencewith "the grandmother couldn’t find," introducing the subject (the grandmother) and the issue (not being able to find something). E follows Bwith "her spectacles because she had," specifying what the grandmother couldn’t find (her spectacles) and hinting at the reason. D comes nextwith "placed them on her head," explaining where the spectacles were, thus completing the thought introduced in E. A follows Dwith "and was searching for them," describing the grandmother's action after placing the spectacles on her head. C concludes the sentencewith "all around the house," indicating where the grandmother was searching, rounding off the sentence with a logical conclusion

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Finishing with six medals, India couldn’t improve on its last Olympics medal _____1_____ of seven. On the medals tally, it slipped from 48th to 70th place. With defending javelin champion Neeraj Chopra _____2_____ silver, the lack of a gold medal cost the country a good 10 places this time. So how will a cold-hearted reviewer — who treats Vinesh Phogat’s tragic 100 grams miss with hard objectivity — see the performance of India’s biggest-ever Olympic _____3____ in 2024? Paris will be remembered for the lessons it taught a nation with grand sporting dreams of producing world-class athletes and hosting mega international events. As the _____4_____ come ______5_____in Paris, Indian sports needs to go into a huddle. The important stake-holders — federations, Sports Authority of India, Sports Ministry and other non-government organisations — need to pull in the same direction. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: B) 'Haul' का use होगा क्योंकि "haul" का अर्थ होता है पुरस्कार या सामान की एक बड़ी मात्रा जिसे एक बार में प्राप्त किया जाता है। यहाँ, sentence में पिछले ओलंपिक में भारत द्वारा प्राप्त पदकों की संख्या का उल्लेख किया गया है, इसलिए 'haul' सही है। जबकि 'Soul' का अर्थ आत्मा है, जो इस context में नहीं फिट बैठता, 'Claim' का अर्थ है दावा करना, जो यहां सही नहीं है, और 'Overhaul' का अर्थ होता है सुधार करना या बदलना, जो medals की संख्या का वर्णन नहीं करता है। 'Haul' will be used because it means a large quantity of items (or medals, in this context) collected at once. The sentence refers to the total medals India won in the last Olympics, making 'haul' appropriate here. In contrast, 'Soul' means spirit, which doesn’t fit, 'Claim' means to assert or demand, which is incorrect here, and 'Overhaul' means to revamp or change, which doesn’t describe a medal count.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Finishing with six medals, India couldn’t improve on its last Olympics medal _____1_____ of seven. On the medals tally, it slipped from 48th to 70th place. With defending javelin champion Neeraj Chopra _____2_____ silver, the lack of a gold medal cost the country a good 10 places this time. So how will a cold-hearted reviewer — who treats Vinesh Phogat’s tragic 100 grams miss with hard objectivity — see the performance of India’s biggest-ever Olympic _____3____ in 2024? Paris will be remembered for the lessons it taught a nation with grand sporting dreams of producing world-class athletes and hosting mega international events. As the _____4_____ come ______5_____in Paris, Indian sports needs to go into a huddle. The important stake-holders — federations, Sports Authority of India, Sports Ministry and other non-government organisations — need to pull in the same direction. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: A) ‘Settling for’ का use होगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ होता है किसी चीज़ को स्वीकार करना, खासकर तब जब यह सर्वोत्तम विकल्प न हो। यहाँ sentence में mention है कि नीरज चोपड़ा ने रजत पदक जीता, जबकि स्वर्ण पदक नहीं मिल सका, इसलिए 'settling for' का उपयोग उचित है। 'Set aside' का अर्थ होता है अलग करना, 'Stand for' का अर्थ होता है समर्थन करना या प्रतिनिधित्व करना, और 'See off' का अर्थ होता है विदाई देना या विदा करना, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं हैं। 'Settling for' will be used because it means to accept something, especially when it’s not the ideal choice. The sentence indicates that Neeraj Chopra won a silver medal instead of gold, making 'settling for' appropriate here. In contrast, 'Set aside' means to keep something separate, 'Stand for' means to represent or support, and 'See off' implies bidding farewell, which do not fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Finishing with six medals, India couldn’t improve on its last Olympics medal _____1_____ of seven. On the medals tally, it slipped from 48th to 70th place. With defending javelin champion Neeraj Chopra _____2_____ silver, the lack of a gold medal cost the country a good 10 places this time. So how will a cold-hearted reviewer — who treats Vinesh Phogat’s tragic 100 grams miss with hard objectivity — see the performance of India’s biggest-ever Olympic _____3____ in 2024? Paris will be remembered for the lessons it taught a nation with grand sporting dreams of producing world-class athletes and hosting mega international events. As the _____4_____ come ______5_____in Paris, Indian sports needs to go into a huddle. The important stake-holders — federations, Sports Authority of India, Sports Ministry and other non-government organisations — need to pull in the same direction. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: B) 'Contingent' का use होगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ होता है किसी विशेष उद्देश्य के लिए चुने गए लोगों का समूह। यहाँ sentence में 2024 ओलंपिक में भारत के सबसे बड़े ओलंपिक दल का उल्लेख किया जा रहा है, इसलिए 'contingent' सबसे उपयुक्त है। 'Stringent' का अर्थ सख्त होता है, 'Diligent' का अर्थ मेहनती होता है, और 'Urgent' का अर्थ तुरंत आवश्यक होता है, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं हैं। 'Contingent' will be used because it refers to a group of people selected for a specific purpose. The sentence refers to India's largest-ever Olympic team in 2024, making 'contingent' the most appropriate choice. In contrast, 'Stringent' means strict, 'Diligent' means hardworking, and 'Urgent' implies something immediately necessary, which do not fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Finishing with six medals, India couldn’t improve on its last Olympics medal _____1_____ of seven. On the medals tally, it slipped from 48th to 70th place. With defending javelin champion Neeraj Chopra _____2_____ silver, the lack of a gold medal cost the country a good 10 places this time. So how will a cold-hearted reviewer — who treats Vinesh Phogat’s tragic 100 grams miss with hard objectivity — see the performance of India’s biggest-ever Olympic _____3____ in 2024? Paris will be remembered for the lessons it taught a nation with grand sporting dreams of producing world-class athletes and hosting mega international events. As the _____4_____ come ______5_____in Paris, Indian sports needs to go into a huddle. The important stake-holders — federations, Sports Authority of India, Sports Ministry and other non-government organisations — need to pull in the same direction. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4
questions.explanation: A) 'Curtains' का use होगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ होता है किसी घटना या प्रदर्शन का अंत। यहां sentence में पेरिस में ओलंपिक समाप्त होने का उल्लेख किया जा रहा है, इसलिए 'curtains' सबसे उपयुक्त है। 'Ruins' का अर्थ होता है खंडहर या नष्ट हुई अवस्था, 'Remains' का अर्थ होता है जो बचा हुआ है, और 'Command' का अर्थ आदेश या नियंत्रण है, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं हैं। 'Curtains' will be used because it signifies the end of an event or performance. The sentence refers to the conclusion of the Olympics in Paris, making 'curtains' the most appropriate choice. In contrast, 'Ruins' means remains or destruction, 'Remains' refers to something left behind, and 'Command' means to order or control, none of which fit in this context.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Finishing with six medals, India couldn’t improve on its last Olympics medal _____1_____ of seven. On the medals tally, it slipped from 48th to 70th place. With defending javelin champion Neeraj Chopra _____2_____ silver, the lack of a gold medal cost the country a good 10 places this time. So how will a cold-hearted reviewer — who treats Vinesh Phogat’s tragic 100 grams miss with hard objectivity — see the performance of India’s biggest-ever Olympic _____3____ in 2024? Paris will be remembered for the lessons it taught a nation with grand sporting dreams of producing world-class athletes and hosting mega international events. As the _____4_____ come ______5_____in Paris, Indian sports needs to go into a huddle. The important stake-holders — federations, Sports Authority of India, Sports Ministry and other non-government organisations — need to pull in the same direction. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5
questions.explanation: D) 'Down'का use होगा क्योंकि "come down" एक phrasal verb है जिसका अर्थ होता है किसी निर्णय या स्थिति पर पहुँच जाना। यहाँ sentence में यह दर्शाने के लिए "come down" का उपयोग किया गया है कि Paris में सीखे गए सबक अब सामने आ रहे हैं, यानी वे अब निष्कर्ष पर आ रहे हैं। इसलिए "come down" उपयुक्त है।'Down' is appropriate because "come down" is a phrasal verb meaning to arrive at a decision or conclusion. In the sentence, it implies that the lessons learned in Paris are now being concluded or brought into focus, making "come down" fitting here. Other options do not fit as they don’t convey this meaning of arriving at a conclusion or outcome.
